summary: Replayed ADR 0101 from the latest `origin/main` in an isolated worktree, recovered the OpenBao runtime after a live Docker network regression during converge, verified the OpenBao and Vaultwarden renewal timers, and recorded the remaining platform drift still surfaced by the shared certificate probe contract.

verification.observed: `tests/test_tls_cert_probe.py`, `tests/test_generate_cert_renewal_config.py`, `tests/test_tls_cert_drift.py`, `tests/test_platform_observation_tool.py`, and `tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py` all passed after adding worktree-aware trust-root resolution plus hour-based probe windows for short-lived certificates.

verification.check: Repository data-model and playbook syntax validation
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, `python3 scripts/generate_cert_renewal_config.py --pretty`, and `make syntax-check-openbao` all completed successfully from the isolated live-apply worktree.

The first `make converge-openbao` replay from current `origin/main` exposed stale production inventory for `postgres-replica-lv3` and an OpenBao Docker publish failure after firewall reload (`lv3-openbao` detached from `openbao_default`). Recovery succeeded by restarting Docker on `docker-runtime-lv3`, removing the broken `lv3-openbao` container, removing `openbao_default`, recreating the stack, and restarting the host-side `lv3-tailscale-proxy-openbao` socket pair.
`lv3-openbao-cert-renew.timer` and `lv3-vaultwarden-cert-renew.timer` both report `Loaded: loaded` and `Active: active (waiting)` on `docker-runtime-lv3`.

`lv3-tailscale-proxy-openbao.socket` and `lv3-tailscale-proxy-step-ca.socket` are active on `proxmox_florin`, and their paired proxy services are running against `10.10.10.20:8200` and `10.10.10.20:9000`.

verification.check: OpenBao certificate probe and mTLS listener behavior
verification.observed: `python3 scripts/tls_cert_probe.py --certificate-id openbao-proxy --fail-on never` reported `status: ok`, issuer `LV3 Internal CA Intermediate CA`, and `hours_remaining: 11`; `curl -vk https://100.64.0.1:8200/v1/sys/health` confirmed the host proxy presents the 24-hour `CN=100.64.0.1` certificate and requests a client certificate on connect.

`curl -vk --resolve vault.lv3.org:443:100.64.0.1 https://vault.lv3.org/` returned HTTP `200` with a 24-hour `CN=vault.lv3.org` certificate from the LV3 internal CA, confirming the service is healthy through the host Tailscale proxy.

verification.check: step-ca proxy health and remaining live drift
verification.observed: `curl --resolve 100.118.189.95:9443:100.64.0.1 --cacert ... https://100.118.189.95:9443/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}`, but `python3 scripts/tls_cert_probe.py --certificate-id step-ca-proxy --fail-on never` still reports `certificate is not valid for '100.64.0.1'` because the live proxy certificate SAN has not yet been rotated from legacy `100.118.189.95`.

notes: The branch intentionally does not modify protected integration files: `VERSION`, release sections in `changelog.md`, the top-level `README.md` integrated status summary, or `versions/stack.yaml`.

The shared certificate probe contract now uses the host Tailscale IP for `vaultwarden-private` while keeping `vault.lv3.org` as SNI, which removes a controller-local private-DNS dependency from the validation path.

The remaining platform issues visible after this replay are unrelated to the ADR 0101 repo implementation itself: stale production inventory for `postgres-replica-lv3` and the legacy SAN on the `step-ca` proxy certificate.
